description: Standard clinic visit with common PHI patterns
text:
NEPHROLOGY CLINIC NOTE

Date of Service: 01/15/2024
Provider: Dr. Mulay

Patient: Margaret Thompson
DOB: 03/22/1958
MRN: 847291
Phone: (740) 555-0173
Address: 142 Maple Street, Zanesville, OH 43701

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Follow-up for CKD Stage 3b.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Thompson is a 65-year-old female with a history of CKD Stage 3b secondary to diabetic nephropathy. She was last seen on 10/12/2023. Her most recent labs from 01/08/2024 show a creatinine of 2.1 mg/dL (eGFR 28 mL/min). She reports compliance with her low-sodium diet and is tolerating lisinopril 20 mg daily without issues. She denies any edema, foamy urine, or changes in urine output.

She was referred by Dr. Patricia Hernandez at Zanesville Family Medicine.

VITALS:
BP: 132/78 mmHg
Weight: 168 lbs
BMI: 28.4

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
1. CKD Stage 3b (eGFR 28) - Stable. Continue current regimen. Recheck BMP in 3 months.
2. Hypertension - Adequately controlled on lisinopril. Continue current dose.
3. Diabetes - A1c 7.2%, at goal. Continue metformin per PCP.

Follow-up in 3 months with Dr. Mulay.

Electronically signed by: Shree Mulay, MD
NPI: 1234567890

HEMODIALYSIS PROGRESS NOTE

Date: 02/20/2024
Provider: Lisa Hoehn, NP
Dialysis Center: Zanesville Dialysis Center, Unit 3

Patient: Robert James Chen
DOB: 11/05/1952
MRN: 663104
SSN: ***-**-4589

DIALYSIS INFORMATION:
Schedule: MWF, 1st shift (6:00 AM)
Access: Left AV fistula
Machine: Fresenius 5008S, Station 12
Dialyzer: Optiflux F180NR
Prescribed time: 4 hours
Blood flow rate: 400 mL/min
Dialysate flow: 800 mL/min
Target UF: 2.5 kg

Pre-dialysis vitals: BP 158/92, HR 78, Temp 97.8F, Weight 84.2 kg
Post-dialysis vitals: BP 138/82, HR 82, Temp 97.6F, Weight 81.7 kg

SESSION NOTES:
Mr. Chen tolerated dialysis well today. No cramping, hypotension, or access issues. Fistula has good thrill and bruit. Kt/V last month was 1.52 (adequate). Labs from 02/15/2024: Hgb 10.8, K 5.1 (pre), Phos 5.8, PTH 285, Albumin 3.6.

Phosphorus remains elevated. Increased Velphoro to 3 tablets with meals. Reinforced dietary phosphorus restriction. Will recheck in 1 month.

Emergency contact: Susan Chen (wife), (740) 555-0291

Signed: Lisa Hoehn, NP

description: Transplant evaluation note with donor information and UNOS ID
text:
KIDNEY TRANSPLANT EVALUATION NOTE

Date: 03/10/2024
Provider: Dr. Anna Lee-Mulay
Transplant Center: Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center

Patient: David Michael Williams
DOB: 07/14/1970
MRN: 990215
UNOS ID: ABCD-1234-5678
Blood Type: O+
Insurance: Medicare #1EG4-TE5-MK72

REASON FOR EVALUATION:
Mr. Williams is a 53-year-old male with ESKD secondary to IgA nephropathy, currently on peritoneal dialysis for 18 months. He is being evaluated for deceased donor kidney transplant listing.

Potential living donor: His sister, Jennifer Williams (DOB 09/22/1973), has completed initial screening and is blood type compatible (O+). She resides at 2847 Oak Ridge Drive, Columbus, OH 43215. Her contact number is (614) 555-0384.

TRANSPLANT WORKUP STATUS:
- Cardiac clearance: Completed 02/28/2024, stress echo normal
- Dental clearance: Completed
- Cancer screening: Up to date (colonoscopy 2023, PSA normal)
- Infectious disease panel: Completed, CMV IgG+, EBV IgG+
- PRA: 12% (low sensitization)
- Crossmatch with living donor: Pending

PLAN:
1. Complete crossmatch testing with sister
2. Present at transplant selection committee 03/25/2024
3. If approved, target living donor transplant in Q2 2024
4. Continue PD in interim, next clinic visit 04/08/2024

Discussed risks, benefits, and alternatives with patient and family.

Electronically signed by: Anna Lee-Mulay, MD

description: Age 90+ patient requiring special HIPAA age handling
text:
NEPHROLOGY CONSULTATION

Date: 04/05/2024
Provider: Leslie Baum, FNP
Facility: Genesis Hospital, Zanesville, OH

Patient: Eleanor Mae Patterson
DOB: 06/18/1931
MRN: 112847
Age: 92 years old

REASON FOR CONSULTATION:
AKI on CKD. Requested by hospitalist Dr. James Morton.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mrs. Patterson is a 92-year-old female admitted on 04/03/2024 for pneumonia. She has a baseline creatinine of 1.4 mg/dL (eGFR 35, CKD Stage 3b). On admission, creatinine was found to be 2.8 mg/dL (eGFR 16). She has been receiving IV fluids and antibiotics (ceftriaxone). Urine output has been adequate at 0.8 mL/kg/hr.

Past medical history includes hypertension, heart failure with preserved EF (55%), osteoarthritis, and CKD Stage 3b. She lives at Primrose Retirement Community, 500 Sunrise Blvd, Zanesville, OH 43701. Her daughter, Carol Patterson-Wright, is her healthcare POA and can be reached at (740) 555-0462.

MEDICATIONS:
Amlodipine 5 mg daily, Furosemide 20 mg daily, Acetaminophen PRN

ASSESSMENT:
1. AKI Stage 2 (KDIGO) on CKD Stage 3b - Likely prerenal from pneumonia/dehydration
2. Avoid nephrotoxins, hold furosemide until volume repleted
3. Recheck BMP in AM
4. Low threshold for renal ultrasound if no improvement in 48 hours

Will follow daily during hospitalization. Discussed prognosis with family - given age and comorbidities, would not recommend dialysis if progresses to ESKD. Family agrees with conservative approach.

Signed: Leslie Baum, FNP
Pager: 740-555-8821
notes: Patient is 90+ years old. Under HIPAA Safe Harbor, ages over 89 must be aggregated to 90+. The exact DOB and age (92) are PHI that need special handling.

description: Multi-patient rounding note with multiple PHI entities
text:
HOSPITAL ROUNDING NOTES - 05/12/2024
Provider: Dr. Shree Mulay
Facility: Genesis Hospital

--- Patient 1 ---
Name: Thomas Andrew Baker
Room: 412-A
MRN: 558901
DOB: 02/28/1965
Admission Date: 05/10/2024

Diagnosis: ESKD on HD, admitted for infected tunneled dialysis catheter
S: Patient reports fevers resolved. Catheter site less erythematous. Blood cultures from 05/10 growing MSSA.
O: Temp 98.6, BP 142/88. Catheter site: minimal erythema, no purulence. HD yesterday via temporary femoral catheter without issues.
A/P: Catheter-related bloodstream infection, improving on nafcillin. Plan to remove tunneled catheter today, continue abx x 4 weeks. IR consulted for new tunneled catheter placement in 1 week. Contact: wife Maria Baker (740) 555-0193.

--- Patient 2 ---
Name: Sandra Lee Kowalski
Room: 418-B
MRN: 774520
DOB: 09/03/1978
Admission Date: 05/11/2024

Diagnosis: Nephrotic syndrome, new diagnosis
S: Patient notes worsening bilateral leg swelling x 2 weeks, foamy urine, 15 lb weight gain. No hematuria.
O: BP 148/94, Weight 187 lbs (baseline 172). 3+ pitting edema bilateral LE. Labs: Albumin 1.8, Cr 1.0, 24hr urine protein 8.2g, Cholesterol 342.
A/P: Nephrotic syndrome - likely membranous nephropathy given age and presentation. Started furosemide 40mg BID, lisinopril 10mg daily. Renal biopsy scheduled 05/14/2024. Discussed with patient and husband, Mark Kowalski, who was present. Their home address is 891 Cherry Lane, Newark, OH 43055. PCP: Dr. Amanda Foster at Licking Memorial.

--- Patient 3 ---
Name: William Henry Jackson
Room: 405
MRN: 331876
DOB: 12/11/1955
Admission Date: 05/09/2024

Diagnosis: Hyperkalemia, CKD Stage 4
S: Feeling better, no palpitations or weakness. Tolerating kayexalate.
O: K+ this AM 5.2 (down from 7.1 on admission). EKG normalized. eGFR 22.
A/P: Hyperkalemia resolving. D/C kayexalate, start patiromer 8.4g daily. Discharge today with follow-up in my office in 1 week. Pharmacy: CVS on Maple Ave, Zanesville. Insurance: Anthem BCBS, ID# XWB123456789.

All patients seen and examined. Notes reviewed with NP Hoehn who is co-managing patients 1 and 3.

Electronically signed: Shree Mulay, MD
